Residential Project

158 Laurel Lane

Deck Replacement — Scope of Work & Material Specifications
Mark Medice  •  412-721-9475
Decking Reference
Pattern Exemplar
Railing Reference
Railing Reference
01
Demolition
  • Remove the entire existing deck surface, joist framing, base structure, and metal drainage system down to the existing footings and support posts
  • Remove all existing railing along the three exposed sides (one 32’ front run and two 21’ side runs)
  • Haul and dispose of all demolition debris from the job site
Note: Existing footings and support posts are to remain in place and be reused.
⚠ HOT TUB SUPPORT:

The existing structural support for the hot tub must be preserved during demolition or reconstructed per the attached engineer’s report. Contractor shall review the engineer’s report prior to beginning any demolition work.

02
Framing & Preparation
  • Inspect existing footings and support posts for structural integrity; notify owner of any concerns before proceeding
  • Verify hot tub support structure is intact and compliant with the attached engineer’s report; reconstruct if necessary per the report’s specifications
  • Install new pressure-treated joists at 12″ on-center spacing to support composite decking
  • Run low-voltage electrical wiring through framing for integrated post and railing lighting
  • Install DekDrain over-joist or under-joist drainage system to create a fully waterproof deck assembly with zero leaks to the area below
Requirement: The space beneath the deck must remain completely dry under all weather conditions.
03
Deck Design & Layout
  • Construct a picture frame-style deck layout with perimeter border boards framing the entire deck surface
  • Divide the deck into two equal framed sections (each approximately 16’ x 21’), separated by a center seam board running the full 32’ width
  • Use TimberTech Vintage Collection Mahogany, TimberTech Vintage Collection Tigerwood, Trex Spice Rum, or equivalent dark-tone composite for all picture frame border boards and the center seam board
  • Install all decking using the TimberTech CONCEALoc Hidden Fastener System (or equivalent) for a clean, screw-free surface
  • Install a lighted railing system on all three exposed sides (one 32’ front run and two 21’ side runs)
  • Coordinate railing post placement and lighting layout with the picture frame borders and center seam board for visual symmetry
04
Installation & Completion
  • Install composite decking per the approved picture frame layout and design specifications above
  • Install railing posts with internal wiring channels for lighting access
  • Install lighted railing system and verify all lighting is fully operational
  • Perform final walkthrough with owner to confirm workmanship and design intent
  • Ensure all work complies with applicable local building codes and is ready for inspection
05
Railing Options Reference

Approved railing systems with integrated lighting for this project’s 74-linear-foot perimeter (32’ front, two 21’ sides). Final selection to be confirmed with owner prior to ordering.

Composite Railing
  • Trex — Post cap lights, rail lights, riser lights; LightHub® plug-and-play system
  • TimberTech (AZEK) — RadianceRail system with LED post caps and under-rail lighting
  • Fiberon — Composite railing with optional integrated lighting accessories
Cable Railing
  • Feeney — DesignRail® aluminum system with LED strip and post accent lights
  • Viewrail — Minimalist cable railing with optional LED lighting
Metal (Aluminum) Railing
  • Westbury — Magena Star LED options including post cap lights and sconces
  • Fortress — Aluminum railing with integrated LED lighting
  • Barrette Outdoor Living — Aluminum railing with optional LED accessories
06
Lighting Options Reference
Integrated Lighting
  • Post Cap Lights — Mounted atop railing posts for ambient downlighting
  • Under-Rail Lights — Mounted beneath the top rail for subtle downward illumination
  • LED Strip Lights — Continuous lighting along the full rail length
Sconce Lighting
  • Side-Mounted Fixtures — Attached to posts or walls for focused, decorative accent lighting

Engineer’s Report Summary

Prepared by Larry Bayles, P.E. — Highland Engineering & Surveying, Inc.
May 18, 2018  •  License #20877, State of Maryland

Proposed Location Assessment

  • Hot tub (6-person, 91″ x 91″) to be installed on first-floor deck over garage roof
  • Existing deck joists observed at approximately 2’ apart, 9″ depth to base
  • Some roof column ends are not properly seated over joists, causing compression of flooring due to insufficient support

Underlying Framing Assessment

  • Central garage beam spans approximately 35’ wall-to-wall, supported by two 6″x6″ wood columns on concrete block
  • Beam consists of (3) 2″x10″ plies nailed together; some butt joints create a weakened condition
  • Concrete floor slab is solid with good bearing capacity at column bases

Load Analysis Critical

  • Estimated hot tub load: approximately 5,086 lbs when filled with water and occupants
  • Design loads applied: 40 PSF live + 10 PSF dead for floor; roof loads at column positions also calculated
  • Garage beam requires reinforcement with two additional plies of LVL (laminated veneer lumber)

Recommendations Action Required

1. Garage Beam Reinforcement:

  • Install two plies of LVL (1-3/4″ x 9-1/4″, 2.0E, 3100Fb) to the existing (3) 2″x10″ beam
  • Reinforce the entire beam even though the left side is not directly affected by hot tub loads
  • Beam ends must be seated on flashing to prevent direct wood-to-concrete contact
  • Connect plies using two rows of 1/4″ x 4-1/2″ or 1/4″ x 6″ heavy-duty connectors at 18″ centers, staggered top and bottom
  • Monitor column bases during work for stress changes in concrete

2. Joist Reinforcement Under Columns:

  • Remove deck flooring and install joist reinforcement under existing columns
  • Create members of 3-ply 2″x10″ No. 1 southern pine or 4-ply 2″x8″ No. 1 southern pine
  • Provide a load transfer path to carry loads to the garage beam below

3. Joist Reinforcement Under Hot Tub:

  • Install 2″x10″ No. 1 southern pine at 12″ spacing from center to center, or (2) 2″x8″ for equivalent support
  • Connect plies with one row of 1/4″ x 3″ heavy-duty connectors
  • All joist additions to be pressure-treated lumber
  • Provide load transfer path to carry loads to the garage beam below

Supporting Documents

  • 2-page findings & recommendations report
  • 2 structural drawings (plan view and framing reinforcement detail)
  • 7 WoodWorks design check calculation sheets covering beam, joist, and LVL analysis

Full report available for download in the Downloads tab.
